seoTitle: Raywood vs Red Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Raywood and Red Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Raywood vs Red Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Raywood and Red Hill.
heading: Raywood vs Red Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Raywood, Victoria and Red Hill, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Raywood has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Red Hill (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Raywood or Red Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Raywood has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Red Hill scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Raywood scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Red Hill scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Raywood or Red Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Raywood has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
